varieties.name: 1. Wahlenbergia stricta 'Blue Mist' 🌱 🌏1. Wahlenbergia stricta 'Blue Mist' 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to all Australian states including Queensland
varieties.traits: Perennial 15-60cm tall, forms tufts, hairless or sparsely haired, alternate leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Multiple flowers on branched stems, reliable bloomer
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent Brisbane adaptation - heat tolerant, humidity resistant, grows well in subtropical conditions. Perfect for rockeries, borders, containers, hanging baskets. Native species found throughout Queensland. Low maintenance, drought tolerant once established. Most commonly encountered native bluebell in Brisbane area. Thrives in subtropical Brisbane climate. Often seen roadside benefiting from extra runoff. Easily propagated by division or root cutting. Forms thin carrot-shaped tubers. Nectar source for butterflies. Excellent for mass plantings, woodland gardens, wildflower meadows. Well-adapted to Brisbane subtropical conditions. Grows in various habitats from woodlands to grasslands. Edible flowers suitable for salads, garnishes, cakes. Tolerates Brisbane's warm humid summers. Previously classified as W. gracilis. Self-seeds readily in suitable conditions. Widespread throughout Brisbane region. Adaptable to subtropical conditions. Found in various habitats from woodlands to open areas. Opens mid-morning in full sunshine, closes late afternoon. Excellent naturalized appearance. Good for cottage gardens, wildflower plantings.
varieties.availability: WT Landcare Group, native seed suppliers, Brisbane native nurseries

varieties.name: 5. Wahlenbergia 'Sky Mist' 🌱 🌏5. Wahlenbergia 'Sky Mist' 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to Queensland and other Australian states
varieties.growing_notes: Ideal for Brisbane subtropical climate - flowers throughout warm months. Perfect for cottage garden style. Drought hardy once established, suitable for Brisbane's variable rainfall. Can be contained in pots or allowed to spread slowly in garden beds. Minimal maintenance required - just remove dead flower stems when unsightly. Well-suited to Queensland conditions including Brisbane. Found in Queensland native habitats. Adapts to subtropical moisture and temperature ranges. Good for naturalized plantings and native gardens.
varieties.availability: Queensland Department of Environment nurseries, native plant specialists

varieties.name: 7. Wahlenbergia queenslandica 🌱 🌏7. Wahlenbergia queenslandica 🌱
varieties.traits: Queensland-specific native, adapted to local conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Seasonal blooming appropriate for Queensland climate
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically adapted to Queensland climate including Brisbane region. Endemic species offering perfect climate match for subtropical conditions. Represents true local provenance for Brisbane gardens. Supports local wildlife and pollinators adapted to native bluebells.
varieties.availability: Queensland native nurseries, specialist suppliers, botanical gardens

varieties.name: 8. Hyacinthoides hispanica 'Excelsior' 🏆8. Hyacinthoides hispanica 'Excelsior'
varieties.traits: Bulbous perennial 30-40cm tall, strappy linear foliage, rigid upright stems
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy flowering in late spring (May in temperate zones)

varieties.name: 1. Plumbago auriculata (Neel Chitrak) 🌱1. Plumbago auriculata (Neel Chitrak)
varieties.origin: Tropical Asia, widely cultivated throughout Kerala
varieties.traits: Evergreen shrub 1.8-2.4m tall, glossy leaves, multiple color varieties
varieties.yield_notes: Year-round blooming, extremely prolific in Kerala climate
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR KERALA - thrives in tropical monsoon climate. Heat and sun loving. Drought-tolerant once established. Perfect for Kerala's hot, humid conditions. Attracts butterflies. Suitable for walls, courtyards, terraces. Traditional medicinal plant. Performs well throughout monsoon and post-monsoon periods. PERFECT FOR KERALA - thrives in monsoon conditions. Traditional Kerala garden plant. Excellent heat tolerance up to 40°C. Loves Kerala's humidity. Sacred flower in Hindu worship (offered to Kali, Durga, Ganesha). Used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine. Attracts pollinators. Requires partial sunlight and well-drained soil. Blooms magnificently June-September during monsoon.
varieties.availability: Every Kerala nursery, Greens of Kerala, widely available throughout Thiruvalla and Kerala

varieties.name: 3. Ixora coccinea (Jungle Geranium) 🌱 🌏3. Ixora coccinea (Jungle Geranium) 🌱

Extremely tough and low-maintenance. Tolerates Kerala's high humidity and heavy monsoon rainfall. Flowers even in full monsoon. Requires minimal fertilizer. Self-seeds readily. Grows well during monsoon and summer. No special care needed. Self-seeds prolifically. Common in every Kerala home garden. EXCELLENT FOR KERALA - indigenous to coastal Kerala. varieties.name: 15. Portulaca grandiflora (Moss Rose) 🌱15. Portulaca grandiflora (Moss Rose)
varieties.origin: South America, widely cultivated in Kerala
varieties.traits: Succulent annual/perennial 10-20cm tall, cylindrical fleshy leaves, spreading growth
varieties.yield_notes: Profuse flowering throughout monsoon season and beyond
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR KERALA - thrives in monsoon conditions. Extremely popular in Kerala for monsoon gardening. Heat and humidity tolerant. Flowers prolifically during rains. Easy from seeds or cuttings. Perfect for containers, hanging baskets, ground cover. Minimal care required. Self-seeds readily in Kerala climate. Opens in full sun, closes at night. Very popular for Kerala balcony and terrace gardens.
varieties.availability: Every Kerala nursery, extremely common and affordable throughout Thiruvalla and Kerala
